key_points: The Earth's crust is made up of various types of rocks, which are natural masses of mineral matter.
Rocks are classified into three major types based on their formation: Igneous, Sedimentary, and Metamorphic.
Igneous rocks (Primary rocks) form when molten magma cools and solidifies.
Sedimentary rocks are formed from the accumulation and compression of sediments (rock fragments) over long periods.
Metamorphic rocks are formed when igneous or sedimentary rocks change their form under great heat and pressure.
examples: Extrusive Igneous: Basalt (Deccan plateau is made of basalt).
Intrusive Igneous: Granite (used for grinding stones for spices/grains).
Sedimentary: Sandstone (made from grains of sand; often contains fossils).
Metamorphic: Clay changing into slate, or limestone changing into marble.
exam_tips: Understand the difference: Extrusive rocks cool quickly on the surface (fine-grained), while Intrusive rocks cool slowly deep inside (large grains).
Remember that fossils are only found in Sedimentary rocks, not in Igneous or Metamorphic.
Note that the Red Fort is made of red sandstone, while the Taj Mahal is made of white marble.
Identify rocks not just as 'stones', but as a source of minerals used in industries and fuel.
